description: Visualization of active site conservation and GO term assessment for Q88K52 AhpC
code:
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import matplotlib.patches as mpatches
import numpy as np

fig, axes = plt.subplots(1, 2, figsize=(16, 8))

# Panel 1: Active site residue conservation
ax1 = axes[0]

residues = ['C47\n(CP)', 'C166\n(CR)', 'T44', 'E50', 'R120']
labels = ['Peroxidatic\nCys', 'Resolving\nCys', 'Active-site\nproximal', 'Active-site\nproximal', 'Active-site\nproximal']

# All residues are conserved across all 3 species
pp_res = ['C', 'C', 'T', 'E', 'R']
ec_res = ['C', 'C', 'T', 'E', 'R']
st_res = ['C', 'C', 'T', 'E', 'R']

x = np.arange(len(residues))
width = 0.25

colors_pp = ['#2ecc71'] * 5  # All green = conserved
colors_ec = ['#3498db'] * 5
colors_st = ['#e74c3c'] * 5

bars1 = ax1.bar(x - width, [1]*5, width, label='P. putida (Q88K52)', color='#2ecc71', alpha=0.8)
bars2 = ax1.bar(x, [1]*5, width, label='E. coli (P0AE08)', color='#3498db', alpha=0.8)
bars3 = ax1.bar(x + width, [1]*5, width, label='S. typhimurium (P0A251)', color='#e74c3c', alpha=0.8)

# Add residue labels on bars
for i, (p, e, s) in enumerate(zip(pp_res, ec_res, st_res)):
    ax1.text(i - width, 0.5, p, ha='center', va='center', fontsize=14, fontweight='bold', color='white')
    ax1.text(i, 0.5, e, ha='center', va='center', fontsize=14, fontweight='bold', color='white')
    ax1.text(i + width, 0.5, s, ha='center', va='center', fontsize=14, fontweight='bold', color='white')

ax1.set_xticks(x)
ax1.set_xticklabels(residues, fontsize=10)
ax1.set_ylabel('Conservation', fontsize=12)
ax1.set_title('Active Site Catalytic Residues\n(All 5/5 Conserved)', fontsize=13, fontweight='bold')
ax1.set_ylim(0, 1.3)
ax1.set_yticks([0, 1])
ax1.set_yticklabels(['Absent', 'Present'])
ax1.legend(fontsize=9, loc='upper right')

# Add a "100% CONSERVED" stamp
ax1.text(2, 1.15, '✓ ALL CATALYTIC RESIDUES INTACT', ha='center', va='center', 
         fontsize=12, fontweight='bold', color='#27ae60',
         bbox=dict(boxstyle='round,pad=0.3', facecolor='#d5f5e3', edgecolor='#27ae60'))

# Panel 2: GO term hierarchy and assessment
ax2 = axes[1]
ax2.set_xlim(0, 10)
ax2.set_ylim(0, 10)
ax2.axis('off')

# Title
ax2.text(5, 9.5, 'GO Term Assessment for Q88K52 (AhpC)', ha='center', fontsize=13, fontweight='bold')

# GO hierarchy
terms = [
    (1, 8.3, 'GO:0051920', 'peroxiredoxin activity', '#27ae60', '✓ APPLIES'),
    (2, 7.3, 'GO:0140824', 'thioredoxin-dependent Prx activity', '#f39c12', '? LIKELY'),
    (3, 6.3, 'GO:0008379', 'thioredoxin peroxidase activity', '#f39c12', '✓ SUPPORTED*'),
    (1, 5.0, 'GO:0102039', 'NADH-dependent Prx activity', '#27ae60', '✓ APPLIES'),
]

for indent, y, go_id, name, color, verdict in terms:
    x_pos = indent * 0.7
    ax2.text(x_pos, y, f'{go_id}', fontsize=9, fontweight='bold', color='#2c3e50')
    ax2.text(x_pos, y - 0.35, f'{name}', fontsize=9, color='#555')
    ax2.text(8.5, y - 0.15, verdict, fontsize=9, fontweight='bold', color=color, ha='center',
             bbox=dict(boxstyle='round,pad=0.2', facecolor=color+'20', edgecolor=color, alpha=0.5))

# Draw hierarchy lines
ax2.annotate('', xy=(1.4, 7.6), xytext=(0.7, 8.0),
             arrowprops=dict(arrowstyle='->', color='gray'))
ax2.annotate('', xy=(2.1, 6.6), xytext=(1.4, 7.0),
             arrowprops=dict(arrowstyle='->', color='gray'))

# Assessment summary
ax2.text(5, 4.0, 'VERDICT: SUPPORTED', ha='center', fontsize=14, fontweight='bold', color='#27ae60',
         bbox=dict(boxstyle='round,pad=0.4', facecolor='#d5f5e3', edgecolor='#27ae60', linewidth=2))

notes = [
    '• Protein is a bona fide 2-Cys peroxiredoxin (AhpC/Prx1 subfamily)',
    '• All catalytic residues intact (C47, C166, T44, E50, R120)',
    '• 69% identity to characterized S. typhimurium AhpC',
    '• "Thioredoxin peroxidase" = recognized synonym for peroxiredoxin',
    '• *Primary reductant is AhpF; thioredoxin use is inferred',
    '• No pseudo-enzyme or mis-placement evidence',
]

for i, note in enumerate(notes):
    ax2.text(0.5, 3.2 - i*0.45, note, fontsize=8.5, color='#2c3e50')

plt.tight_layout()
plt.savefig('active_site_and_go_assessment.png', dpi=150, bbox_inches='tight')
plt.show()
print("Figure saved: active_site_and_go_assessment.png")
